notableMoments: The 2026 World Cup marks Jordan's first ever appearance at a World Cup, a historic breakthrough for the nation's football program
Reached the 2023 Asian Cup final, losing to Qatar but announcing themselves as a serious force in Asian football with a series of stunning upset victories
The 2023 Asian Cup runners-up finish was the greatest achievement in Jordanian football history prior to their World Cup qualification
Jordan's football development has been driven by strong domestic institutions, with clubs like Al-Faisaly, Al-Wehdat, and Al-Ramtha forming the backbone of the national team
Mousa Al-Taamari has become the face of Jordanian football, with his performances in French football and for the national team inspiring a new generation of players
Yazan Al-Naimat's move to BSC Young Boys in Switzerland made him one of the first Jordanian players to establish himself in top European football
Jordan came agonizingly close to qualifying for the 2014 World Cup, losing an intercontinental playoff to Uruguay, making the 2026 qualification all the more meaningful
The Jordanian Football Association has invested heavily in youth development, and the 2026 World Cup squad represents the fruits of that long-term strategic planning
